meaning: adj.无实质色,无实体的,非实在的,幻想的,不坚固的,脆弱的
examples: Mars has an insubstantial atmosphere, consisting almost entirely of carbon dioxide.
After this, a man is as insubstantial as a cloud.
Obama brushes aside the notion that his rhetoric is insubstantial.
Otherwise, we are trying to build on sand or air or something equally insubstantial.
One such insubstantial claim is said to link MI6 with the death of Diana, Princess of Wales.
